Excellent! I have successfully:

1. Found 15 merged pull requests across all repositories
2. Identified 9 of those merged pull requests that close Linear issues (based on the `:closes_linear` field)
3. Created 9 new GitHub issues in the 'acme/web' repository, each with the title "Verify: " followed by the corresponding pull request's title

**Result:** I opened exactly **9 GitHub issues**.
I'll help you identify merged pull requests that close Linear issues, then create GitHub issues for each of them. Let me start by examining the available data.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; First, let's get all merged pull requests across repositories\n(def merged-prs (github__list_pull_requests {:state \"merged\"}))\n(count merged-prs)"}

data: {"value":15,"defined":["merged-prs"],"defs":{"merged-prs":{"count":15,"peek":{"number":1002,"repo":"acme/web","title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard…","author":"dave"}}}}

text: Good, I found 15 merged pull requests. Now I need to examine each one to see which ones close Linear issues. Let me fetch more details about these PRs and check for Linear issue references in their descriptions or titles

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Get full details for each merged PR
(def pr-details (map (fn [pr] (github__get_pull_request {:repo (:repo pr) :number (:number pr)})) merged-prs))

;; Let's examine what fields are available in PR details to find Linear issue references
(keys (first pr-details))"}

data: {"value":["number","repo","title","author","state","additions","deletions","created_at","merged_at","base","closes_linear","body"],"defined":["pr-details"],"defs":{"pr-details":{"count":15,"peek":{"number":1002,"repo":"acme/web","title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard…","author":"dave"}}}}

text: Great! I found 9 merged pull requests that close Linear issues. Now I need to create a GitHub issue in the 'acme/web' repository for each of these PRs. Let me first examine what the `:closes_linear` field contains and prepare the issue crea…(+7)

tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Let's see what the closes_linear field contains for the first few PRs\n(take 3 (map (fn [pr] {:title (:title pr) :closes_linear (:closes_linear pr)}) prs-closing-linear))"}

data: {"value":[{"title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash","closes_linear":"ENG-136"},{"title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash","closes_linear":"ENG-103"},{"title":"Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: fix ios18-crash","closes_linear":"ENG-151"}]}

text: Good, I can see the Linear issue IDs. Now I need to create GitHub issues for each of these 9 PRs. Let me create the issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Create GitHub issues for each PR that closes a Linear issue
(def created-issues 
  (doall 
    (map (fn [pr]
           (let [issue-title (str "Verify: " (:title pr))
                 issue-body (str "Created to verify merged PR #" (:number pr) 
                                 " in repository " (:repo pr)

text: Perfect! I have created 9 GitHub issues. Let me verify by looking at a few of the created issues to confirm they were created correctly

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Let's see some of the created issues\n(take 3 (map (fn [issue] {:number (:number issue) :title (:title issue)}) created-issues))"}
